codeReview.summary: The original fix commit a7b08e0 only strengthened VAL-CROSS-005 proof and correctly failed review, but the subsequent follow-up commit 45e0674 now completes the missing structural work by extracting the timer-page orchestration into useTimerPageOrchestration while preserving the shared /timer page-family behavior. WorkspaceTimerPage is reduced to a presenter, the orchestration boundary is explicit in the new hook, and the duplicate status-block traceability issue called out in the prior scrutiny round is also corrected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The original fix worker still marked followedProcedure true without the required agent-browser replay for a browser-visible timer change; the later extraction follow-up did invoke agent-browser, but the assigned feature handoff itself did not follow the tracking-frontend-worker procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tracking-frontend-worker SKILL.md step 11 requires an agent-browser replay. The reviewed worker session cc856035-d35e-4b1c-90ee-46b0a2038134 reports verification.interactiveChecks as [] in its handoff, and the transcript skeleton for that session shows no agent-browser invocation. The later follow-up session 8be79e30-a74f-48fe-aa3b-33c53fd79633 did invoke agent-browser before commit 45e0674 landed the actual extraction.

summary: Review passed after considering the follow-up fix evidence. Commit 45e0674 delivers the previously missing orchestration extraction into useTimerPageOrchestration, keeps WorkspaceTimerPage as a thin presenter, and removes the duplicate mission status blocks that were part of the same scrutiny round.
